rationale: Explicit Euler is unstable for stiff, diffusion-dominated Allen-Cahn unless dt is extremely small; low spatial resolution further limits accuracy. Efficiency is high, but stability and accuracy are poor.
Crank-Nicolson is stable and second-order accurate for stiff diffusion; medium spatial resolution is reasonable. Computational cost is moderate and overall suitability is good.
IMEX BDF2 is well-suited for stiff diffusion and nonlinear reaction; high spatial resolution and second-order accuracy provide robust, accurate results. Cost is higher but justified by accuracy.
Implicit backward Euler is robust for stiff problems, but only first-order accurate in time and space (linear FEM). Medium resolution is adequate, but accuracy is lower than higher-order schemes.
Quadratic FEM with high resolution and IMEX Runge-Kutta (third order) offers excellent stability and accuracy for stiff, nonlinear Allen-Cahn. Computational cost is high but justified.
Spectral methods are highly accurate spatially, but explicit RK4 is unstable for stiff diffusion unless dt is extremely small, making this plan inefficient for Allen-Cahn.
High-resolution Chebyshev spectral method with IMEX BDF3 provides excellent stability and accuracy for stiff, nonlinear problems. Computational cost is high but optimal for accuracy.
Implicit backward Euler is stable, but first-order FVM with piecewise constant reconstruction is low-accuracy. Medium resolution is reasonable, but accuracy is limited.
IMEX RK2 is stable, but low spatial resolution and first-order FVM limit accuracy. Efficiency is high, but overall suitability is low for resolving sharp interfaces in Allen-Cahn.
Semi-implicit Euler is robust for stiff diffusion, but only first-order accurate in time. Medium spatial resolution and second-order FD are reasonable; accuracy is moderate.
